summary: Iteration 7 (per-document chat) testing complete. BACKEND: 13/13 pytest green — happy-path on-topic answer references the letter, off-topic code request returns off_topic=true with no def/return/for-loop, prompt-injection returns off_topic=true with no pirate joke, safety question stays on-topic and recommends contacting the sender, history accumulates user/assistant pairs in order, DELETE clears messages and subsequent GET returns [], cross-device chat/list both 404, empty/whitespace/>2000-char messages return 400, unknown analysis_id chat returns 404. /api/, /api/languages regression both green. FRONTEND: chat-screen, chat-scope-banner, all 4 chat-starter-{0..3}, chat-input, chat-send all RENDER, and result.tsx has the result-ask-question button which navigates to /chat?id=. CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG: the English (default) i18n dictionary is MISSING all 10 iter-7 strings — every chat_* key plus ask_question. This causes empty button/banner/starter labels AND a hard RED SCREEN CRASH ('Cannot read properties of undefined (reading trim)') when an English user taps any chat-starter, because send(undefined) is invoked. zh, vi, tr, ru, es all have the keys — only en is missing them. This blocks the chat feature for the default-language user.

critical.issue: Returns 404 when the analysis exists but has never had a chat (no `messages` field yet). Root cause in server.py list_messages(): `find_one({...}, {"_id":0, "messages":1})` returns `{}` (empty dict) when the document has no `messages` field — and `if not doc:` treats `{}` as falsy → 404. Reproduces on every fresh analysis: open /chat?id=<fresh_id> -> frontend Promise.all -> listChatMessages 404 -> Alert(error_generic). Fix: change `if not doc:` to `if doc is None:` (or include `id` in the projection so the dict is never empty).

ui_bugs.issue: RED SCREEN: English dictionary is missing iter-7 keys: ask_question, chat_placeholder, chat_thinking, chat_clear_confirm, chat_scope_note, chat_empty_title, chat_empty_sub, chat_starter_1, chat_starter_2, chat_starter_3, chat_starter_4. All 5 other languages have them. Tapping a starter chip when lang='en' throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading trim)' at app/chat.tsx:85 because t(lang,'chat_starter_1') is undefined and Pressable onPress passes undefined to send(text), which then calls text.trim().
Renders with an empty label for English users because t('en','ask_question') is undefined. Button still navigates correctly to /chat?id=... but has no visible text.

ui_bugs.component: app/chat.tsx scope banner / empty state / placeholder / thinking bubble
ui_bugs.issue: All these copy strings render empty for English users (chat_scope_note, chat_empty_title, chat_empty_sub, chat_placeholder, chat_thinking, chat_clear_confirm). Banner is visible but its inner Text is empty.

integration_issues.flow: Tap starter chip -> send chat message
integration_issues.issue: App crashes (red dev-error overlay) instead of sending. Reproduces 100% with localStorage klarpost.language='en' (the default) on /chat?id=<seeded>. Workaround: typing into chat-input + tapping chat-send works because input state is a real string.

react_native_issues.issue: send(text: string) does not defensively guard against an undefined text. A simple 'const trimmed = (text || "").trim();' (or 'text?.trim() ?? ""') would have prevented the red screen even when i18n is incomplete. Defensive fix recommended in addition to filling in the en strings.

action_items:
Add the 10 missing keys to the `en` dict in /app/frontend/src/i18n.ts (between line 232 and the closing `};` at 233). Suggested values:
  ask_question: 'Ask about this document',
  chat_placeholder: 'Ask a question about this letter…',
  chat_thinking: 'Thinking…',
  chat_clear_confirm: 'Clear this conversation?',
  chat_scope_note: 'I can only help with this document.',
  chat_empty_title: 'Ask anything about this letter',
  chat_empty_sub: 'I can explain words, deadlines, next steps, and how to reply politely — only for this document.',
  chat_starter_1: 'Explain this letter in simple words',
  chat_starter_2: 'What should I do next?',
  chat_starter_3: 'What happens if I miss the deadline?',
  chat_starter_4: 'How can I reply politely?'
Harden app/chat.tsx send() to coerce text safely: const trimmed = (text ?? '').trim(); to avoid red-screen crashes if any starter ever resolves to undefined.
After fix, re-run: tap each chat-starter-{0..3} on /chat?id=<seed> with lang='en'. Expect optimistic user bubble + 'Thinking…' bot bubble + real reply (not red screen).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/analyses/{id}/chat correctly enforces ownership via doc filter {id, device_id}, soft-cap of 80 user turns (HTTP 429), 1-2000 char message validation, and stores user+assistant messages atomically with $push $each. Off-topic flag round-trips correctly.

build_chat_system_prompt embeds DOCUMENT_CONTEXT_JSON of the trimmed analysis (key arrays sliced to 8-12 items) — small, safe, and fits well within the model's context. System prompt explicitly enumerates refusal categories and demands JSON output {reply, off_topic}.

chat_about_document bakes the last 12 history turns into the user message rather than relying on the LLM session — robust against cross-process state loss. Falls back to raw text if the model output isn't valid JSON (off_topic defaults to false in that case, which is acceptable).

i18n.ts uses TypeScript `UIStrings` type to require all keys — but the `const en: UIStrings = { ... };` literal at lines 136-233 was clearly copy-pasted before iter-7 keys were added, and TypeScript strict mode is not catching this (likely metro runtime, not tsc). Adding a `pretsc`/`tsc --noEmit` step in CI would have caught this.
chat.tsx send() unconditionally calls text.trim() on the parameter. Should be hardened to `(text ?? '').trim()` to be safe against future typos/undefined translations.

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 in test_iter7_chat.py: 2 regression + 4 scope/safety + 4 history/ownership + 4 validation/404). LLM cost kept low by reusing one seeded analysis across the module via pytest module-scoped fixture; only ~5 chat calls were made total.
success_rate.frontend: Partial — chat-screen, chat-scope-banner, chat-starter-0..3, chat-input, chat-send, result-ask-question all render and navigation /result -> /chat works. BUT tapping any starter chip with lang='en' throws a red-screen crash (root cause: missing en translations -> send(undefined) -> .trim() on undefined). Manual workaround using TextInput would work but tests should be re-run after main agent fixes en translations.

seed_data_creation: Backend tests: TEST_chat-test-be device id, one analysis seeded per module via /api/analyze with synthetic AOK Mahnung JPEG, cleaned up via DELETE /api/analyses?device_id=. Frontend: device_id 'chat-fe-test' with one analysis seeded out-of-band via curl to /api/analyze (id=doc-assistant-app). Not cleaned up — main agent can hit DELETE /api/analyses?device_id=chat-fe-test to remove.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 7 backend chat is solid and verified by /app/backend/tests/test_iter7_chat.py (13 tests, 33s with LLM). The single OUTSTANDING issue is purely on the frontend: the `en` i18n dict in /app/frontend/src/i18n.ts (lines 136-233) is missing 10 keys: ask_question + chat_* (placeholder, thinking, clear_confirm, scope_note, empty_title, empty_sub, starter_1..4). All 5 other languages have them. This causes English starter chips to render with empty text AND crash on tap with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading trim)' at chat.tsx:85. Fix = paste the 10 keys into the en dict (suggested values in action_items). After fix, regression-test by setting localStorage.klarpost.language='en' and tapping chat-starter-0 on /chat?id=<seed>. Backend regression passes /api/, /api/languages, /api/analyze, /api/analyses (not retested in this iteration but iter-5 suite remains green).
rca_of_issue: RCA: app/chat.tsx introduces 11 new UI strings (ask_question reused from result + 10 chat_*). The author added them to the `UIKey` type union (lines 122-132) and to all 5 non-English language dicts (zh, vi, tr, ru, es) but forgot to add them to the `en` dict (lines 136-233). TypeScript should have flagged 'Property chat_* is missing in type ...' but the metro/expo dev pipeline does not run `tsc --noEmit`, so the omission shipped to runtime. Reproduction: localStorage.klarpost.language='en' (default), open /chat?id=<analysis>, tap any chat-starter-N. Expected: message sends. Actual: red dev overlay 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading trim)' at app/chat.tsx:85 inside send(text). Mitigation: (1) add the 10 missing en strings, (2) defensively coerce in send(): `const trimmed = (text ?? '').trim();`, (3) add a CI step running `tsc --noEmit` on /app/frontend so future i18n drift is caught at build time.
